active Encounter 12/02/2025 SEEN BY SEEN ON Irfan Tahir MD 12/02/2025 HEIGHT WEIGHT BMI BLOOD PRESSURE 71.0 in 278.0 lbs 38.8 144/82 TEMP PULSE RESP RATE HEAD CIRC N/A 85.0 bpm N/A N/A CC 2-3 WK F/U, LAB RESULTS. S Patient mentioned above is a 86-year-old male who is here to follow-up on his labs. His most recent A1c is 8.5. His last A1c was 8.4. Suspect noncompliance Constitutional: Patient denies having any fever or chills. Patient denies night sweats or fatigue. No significant weight changes. ENT/mouth: Patient denies having any recent hearing changes. Denies otalgia no nasal congestion or sinus pain. No hoarseness or sore throat or any dysphagia. Eyes: Denies having any pain in the eye. No swelling or redness. No foreign body. Denies having any discharge or any visual/vision changes. Cardiovascular: denies having any chest pain or shortness of breath. No dyspnea on exertion or orthopnea. No palpitations or diaphoresis. Respiratory: Denies having any cough or sputum production. No wheezing. No shortness of breath on exertion or at rest Gastrointestinal: Denies having any nausea vomiting or diarrhea. No change in bowel habits. No blood in stool. No recent heartburn. No weight loss. Genitourinary: Patient denies having any urinary incontinence or urgency. Denies having any flank pain. No urinary changes in color no urinary flow changes or hesitancy. Musculoskeletal: Patient denies having any arthralgias myalgias or any joint swelling. Denies having any joint stiffness. Denies having any neck or back pain. No recent history of injury. Skin no new skin lesions: Patient denies having any pruritus or any hair changes. No changes in the breast. Denies having any type of discharge. Neuro: Denies having any headaches weakness numbness or paresthesias. No loss of consciousness. Denies having any syncopal episodes. No coordination changes. No recent falls. Psychiatry: Denies having an anxiety or panic attacks. No depression or insomnia. No social issues. No memory changes. No history of violence or history of drug abuse. Heme, lymph: No bruising bleeding or recent history of transfusions. Denies having any lymphadenopathy. Endocrine: denies having any polyuria or polydipsia. No temperature intolerance. No signs or symptoms of any pathology related to the thyroid. O General: WN WD, NAD. ENT: Pupils are equally round and reactive to light and accommodation. Extraocular movements are equally intact bilaterally. Neck: Trachea midline Chest: Normal respiratory effort lungs are clear to auscultation bilaterally. No sensory muscle use. Heart: Regular rate and rhythm negative for any murmurs. From Irfan Tahir 17025416081 12/2/2025 11:49:13 PST Page 5 of 6 Abdomen: Soft nontender no peritoneal signs or symptoms. Bowel sounds are positive. Extremities: No clubbing cyanosis or edema Neurologic: Intact, no obvious cranial nerve abnormalities Psychiatry: Mood and affect are appropriate, speech is clear Musculoskeletal: Gait is normal A Diagnoses attached to this encounter: (I10) Essential (primary) hypertension (E78.2) Mixed hyperlipidemia (E11.65) Type 2 diabetes mellitus with hyperglycemia (G47.33) Obstructive sleep apnea (adult) (pediatric) (E83.119) Hemochromatosis, unspecified (170.90) Unspecified atherosclerosis P Labs discussed with the patient Continue present management continue to monitor blood pressure at home salt restricted diet, please diet and exercise to maintain a healthy weight, and decrease stress please avoid alcohol caffeine and nicotine. Advised the patient of the DASH diet which basically includes to have grains 6-8 servings a day vegetables 4-5 servings a day fruits with 5 servings a day lean meat poultry and fish and nuts seeds and legumes 4-5 servings a week Discussed/continue statin. Advised the patient to focus on foods low in saturated and trans-fats such as a variety of fruits and vegetables, a variety of whole grain foods such as whole grain bread, cereal, past and brown rice, at least half of the serving should be whole grains. Skinless poultry and lean meats. Fatty fish such as salmon, Trout, Albacore tuna and sardines. Enjoy at least 8 ounces of non- fried fish each week. unsalted nuts, seeds and legumes. Usage of nontropical vegetable always like canola, corn, olive, or sunflower orals. Please be advised to limit foods with a high content of sodium sweets and sugars within beverages red meat and fatty meats that are not trimmed processed meats such as salami and sausage. Also limit the intake of full fat dairy products such as whole milk, cream, ice cream butter and cheese please avoid fried foods, will repeat a cholesterol level in 4-6 months. Discussed with the patient his underlying diabetes in detail. Continue to monitor A1c every 4 months. Advised ADA diet 1800 cal. Advised yearly eye exam and monthly for exam. Patient to continue home blood sugar monitoring. Patient to continue to diet and exercise. Advised the patient to monitor his blood sugar at least twice a day. And call the physician if blood sugars are constantly higher than 200 and lower than 60. Discussed with the patient that obesity is a condition resulting from accumulation of excess body fat and this is becoming a major health concern which increases the risk of several other health issues. The diseases that can be caused by obesity include diabetes, heart disease, arthritis, cancer, liver problems, infertility and many more it is of upmost importance that your BMI be less than 30. Foods that can be consumed are as follows wheat, brown rice, brown bread, oatmeal, quinoa, barley, pulses Black current peach apple pear papaya, oranges, lemon, guava and pomegranate You can consume skimmed milk, butter milk, cottage cheese Green leafy vegetables like spinach, green onions, carrots, beet roots, tomatoes, reddish, Oka, cabbage, broccoli, cauliflower, mushroom, zucchini, beans, cucumber, garlic and ginger black beans and most of the lentils are good for obesity Green tea homemade vegetable juices and coconut water and herbal tea Lean meat chicken Salmom and egg white olive oil and canola oil Foods to be avoided white rice, white flour, white bread, pasta, mango, banana, potato and sweet potatoes dried and frozen pulses butter cheese and coconut milk do not consume beverages such as soda, cold drinks, alcohol, and energy drinks, fruit syrups also avoid corn oil, sunflower oil, rice bran oil and cotton seed oil Do not skip meals eat 3-4 balanced meals avoid intake of fatty and sugary food eat seasonal fruits and vegetables. Avoid late night snacking increase fiber intake she was a low-calorie food and increase physical activities also drink 6-8 glasses of water daily. From Irfan Tahir 17025416081 12/2/2025 11:49:13 PST Page 6 of 6 Avoid grocery shopping when you are hungry. Pack a healthy lunch instead of eating out of control, and always control, to eat. Aim for 150 minutes per week of exercise Be more active, small changes can easily be added to your daily routine for example take the stairs instead of the elevator Take a walk with the family or the dog. A daily walk is a great way to get exercise and to catch up on the day's events Continue follow-up with endocrinology. SIGNED BY This encounter is not yet electronically signed. Referral electronically submitted by Irfan Tahir MD 12/02/2025 11:48AM practice fusion
